text: Fill in the blanks to test your knowledge of molecular biology fundamentals.
questions: The two purine bases found in DNA are *adenine/Adenine/A* and *guanine/Guanine/G*, which have a *double/two*-ring structure.
The enzyme *helicase/DNA helicase* unwinds the DNA double helix during replication, while *DNA polymerase/polymerase* synthesises the new strand in the *5' to 3'/5 to 3* direction.
The final degradation product of purine metabolism in humans is *uric acid/urate*, and its accumulation in joints causes *gout/Gout*.
In *transcription/Transcription*, the enzyme *RNA polymerase* reads the template DNA strand and synthesises a complementary *mRNA/messenger RNA* molecule.
*CRISPR/CRISPR-Cas9* is a genome editing technology derived from bacterial immune systems, while *PCR/polymerase chain reaction* is used to amplify specific DNA sequences for diagnosis.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
